checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?
Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill end 2015-12-31 = 24,643,994.00; end 2016-03-31 = 77,185,639.49 (3.1x increase in one quarter)

Total Assets grew from 311,199,401.36 (2015-12-31) to 405,779,858.50 (2016-03-31), a 30.4% single-quarter jump, while revenue grew only 30.9% YoY (not sequentially) - consistent with a large acquisition inflating soft assets disproportionately to organic revenue growth

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss annual 2015 = 67,422,377.35 vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities annual 2015 = 43,062,305.60 (NI exceeds OCF by ~$24.4M)

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: SalesRevenueGoodsNet Q1 2016 = 122,194,186.37 vs Q1 2015 = 93,344,021.51 (+30.9% YoY), far below the inventory growth rate

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss Q4 2015 (2015-10-01 to 2015-12-31) = 30,631,548.06 vs Q1 2016 (2016-01-01 to 2016-03-31) = 2,740,765.66, a ~91% sequential decline despite continued revenue growth
evidence.location: 10-Q Q1 2016 income statement / 10-K FY2015 comparative quarterly data

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AccruedLiabilitiesCurrent grew from 5,733,397.84 (2014-12-31) to 10,633,346.48 (2015-12-31) to 11,762,166.42 (2016-03-31), tracking growth in business activity

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: NT 10-Q filed 2009-02-18 prior to the corresponding 10-Q
10-KT (transition period 10-K) filed 2010-03-30 indicating a fiscal year change, and an 8-K/A filed 2016-03-31 shortly before the cutoff date

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Financial series span quarterly and annual data from 2009-12-31 through 2016-03-31 across Assets, Revenue, Receivables, Inventory, Cash Flow, and Equity concepts

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Potential premature or aggressive revenue recognition and/or extension of lenient credit terms, causing receivables to build up faster than sales (rising DSO), which could indicate channel stuffing or recognition of revenue ahead of cash collectability.

rationale_evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent grew 101.6% YoY (61,070,761.69 to 123,082,588.27) in FY2015 while SalesRevenueGoodsNet grew only 58.4% YoY (270,694,685.77 to 428,850,831.61)
AccountsReceivableNetCurrent grew 73.8% YoY into Q1 2016 (70,982,854.04 to 123,373,379.62) while SalesRevenueGoodsNet grew only 30.9% YoY (93,344,021.51 to 122,194,186.37)

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Reported net income may be inflated relative to underlying cash generation, potentially through non-cash revenue recognition (e.g., recognizing revenue on receivables not yet collected) or timing of expense recognition, producing a growing gap between accrual earnings and operating cash flow.

rationale_evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss annual 2015 = 67,422,377.35 exceeded NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities annual 2015 = 43,062,305.60 by approximately $24.4 million

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: A large acquisition apparently closed in Q1 2016 (goodwill jumped from $9.25M to $70.36M and intangibles from $24.6M to $77.2M in one quarter) alongside a simultaneous sharp drop in net income; risk of aggressive purchase price allocation (overvaluing intangibles/goodwill, undervaluing assumed liabilities) and/or future goodwill impairment risk that has not yet been reflected in reported earnings.

rationale_evidence.quote: InventoryNet increased from 17,081,129.36 (2015-12-31) to 41,138,961.28 (2016-03-31), a 140.8% single-quarter jump
NetIncomeLoss fell from 30,631,548.06 (Q4 2015) to 2,740,765.66 (Q1 2016), coincident with the acquisition-driven balance sheet expansion

top_signals: Accounts receivable growing significantly faster than revenue in FY2015 (101.6% vs 58.4%) and into Q1 2016 (73.8% YoY vs 30.9% YoY), indicating rising DSO and potential revenue recognition/collectability concerns
Net income exceeding operating cash flow in FY2015 by ~$24.4M and operating cash flow turning negative in Q1 2016 despite positive net income, indicating a growing earnings-to-cash-flow gap

Sudden large acquisition-like balance sheet expansion in Q1 2016 (goodwill 7.6x, intangibles 3.1x, inventory +141%, total assets +30% sequentially) coincident with a ~91% sequential collapse in net income, raising purchase accounting and integration/earnings-quality risk
History of filing amendments and a late filing notice (NT 10-Q) plus multiple 10-K/A and 8-K/A amendments suggesting recurring reporting/control issues
